definition: 2-4 month directional changes in market delivery. Detected via weekly return analysis — when the trend of weekly closes reverses, a quarterly shift has occurred.
depends_on: IPDA (quarterly shifts occur when IPDA 60-day range is exhausted)
feeds_into: market_structure (quarterly shift changes the HTF bias)
related_to: seasonal_tendencies (quarterly shifts align with seasonal patterns)
dealing_range (new quarterly range establishes new dealing range)
detection: Analyze 8-12 weekly closes. When 3+ consecutive weekly closes reverse the prior quarterly direction, a shift is underway.
trading_implication: A quarterly shift changes the macro bias. Trades aligned with the new quarterly direction have significantly higher probability. Counter-quarterly trades should be avoided or sized down.
typical_timing: Shifts often align with fiscal quarters: Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct. But can occur at any time when IPDA 60-day range extremes are tested.
